channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Memphis Grizzlies
channel.description: Memphis Grizzlies trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Grizzlies were going to pursue Ja Morant trade no matter the lottery outcome
item.description: Tim MacMahon: “I can just say the lottery results will have no impact on the approach moving forward with Ja Morant. They’re going to try to find a home for Ja Morant regardless of where they ended up in the lottery.”

item.title: It didn't take long for an agent on the ground in …
item.description: It didn't take long for an agent on the ground in Chicago to suggest to me that Memphis could potentially look to trade down from No. 3 as part of deal that ships out Morant, but I find such a scenario almost certainly too drastic. If anything Grizzlies president Zach Kleiman has a history of trading up and/or into the draft. Don't forget that he just did so last June, swapping picks with Portland to be able to select Cedric Coward at No. 11 overall.

item.title: Utah actually won the No. 3 pick after popping up …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Emerging from the sequestered lottery room to report Utah actually won the No. 3 pick after popping up second as well. So, the league had to toss out that outcome and draw again… which led to the Grizzlies claiming the third pick.

item.title: Wizards win 2026 draft lottery
item.description: Shams Charania: Top 4 order in the 2026 NBA Draft: 1. Washington Wizards 2. Utah Jazz 3. Memphis Grizzlies 4. Chicago Bulls

item.title: Wizards, Jazz, Grizzlies and Bulls make the top four in the draft lottery
item.description: Josh Robbins: The Wizards, Jazz, Grizzlies and Bulls are the teams that will draft somewhere in the top four.

item.title: Grizzlies were going to pursue Ja Morant trade no matter the lottery outcome
item.description: Tim MacMahon: “I can just say the lottery results will have no impact on the approach moving forward with Ja Morant. They’re going to try to find a home for Ja Morant regardless of where they ended up in the lottery.”

item.title: It didn't take long for an agent on the ground in …
item.description: It didn't take long for an agent on the ground in Chicago to suggest to me that Memphis could potentially look to trade down from No. 3 as part of deal that ships out Morant, but I find such a scenario almost certainly too drastic. If anything Grizzlies president Zach Kleiman has a history of trading up and/or into the draft. Don't forget that he just did so last June, swapping picks with Portland to be able to select Cedric Coward at No. 11 overall.

item.title: Utah actually won the No. 3 pick after popping up …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Emerging from the sequestered lottery room to report Utah actually won the No. 3 pick after popping up second as well. So, the league had to toss out that outcome and draw again… which led to the Grizzlies claiming the third pick.

item.title: Wizards win 2026 draft lottery
item.description: Shams Charania: Top 4 order in the 2026 NBA Draft: 1. Washington Wizards 2. Utah Jazz 3. Memphis Grizzlies 4. Chicago Bulls

item.title: Wizards, Jazz, Grizzlies and Bulls make the top four in the draft lottery
item.description: Josh Robbins: The Wizards, Jazz, Grizzlies and Bulls are the teams that will draft somewhere in the top four.

item.title: Brandon Clarke dead at 29 years old
item.description: Michael Scotto: Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke has passed away at 29 years old. Clarke was a first-round draft pick in 2019 and was an All-Rookie First Team selection. He averaged 10.2 points and 5.5 rebounds in seven NBA seasons for Memphis. Thoughts and prayers to his loved ones 🙏🏼

item.title: Statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke
item.description: Priority Sports: Statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke.

item.title: During this difficult time, the family kindly asks for …
item.description: Priority Sports: During this difficult time, the family kindly asks for privacy as they grieve and make arrangements. Your thoughts, prayers, and understanding are greatly appreciated.

item.title: Grizzlies release statement on tragic passing of Brandon Clarke
item.description: Memphis Grizzlies: Another statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke.

item.title: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver issued the following …
item.description: NBA Communications: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver issued the following statement today regarding the passing of Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke.

item.title: Brandon Clarke's death being investigated as possible drug overdose
item.description: Brandon Clarke's death is being investigated as a possible overdose ... with officials finding drug paraphernalia inside the home the Memphis Grizzlies player was staying at on Monday. The Los Angeles Fire Department responded to a medical emergency call around 5 PM on Monday ... and when paramedics got to the scene, Clarke was declared deceased, according to NBC4.

item.title: In 2019 we headed to Summer League, and the kid out of …
item.description: Chris Vernon: in 2019 we headed to Summer League, and the kid out of Gonzaga they drafted 21st overall won the MVP. He became beloved from literally the first time he wore the uniform. Look up and 7 years later he is gone. Still can’t believe it. So intensely sad. RIP BC 😢

item.title: Ja Morant remembers Brandon Clarke
item.description: Davide Fumagalli: Ja Morant remembers Brandon Clarke

item.title: NBPA releases statement on the passing of Brandon Clarke
item.description: The NBPA: Statement from NBPA Executive Director Andre Iguodala and NBPA President Fred VanVleet on the tragic passing of Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke.

item.title: "He was an extended brother."
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "He was an extended brother." Danny Green and the NBA Today crew took a moment to share a message on Brandon Clarke.

item.title: kyle anderson on brandon clarke
item.description: kyle anderson on brandon clarke 😥

item.title: “One of my favorite teammates and people ever 💔 fly …
item.description: Lamar Stevens on Brandon Clarke: “One of my favorite teammates and people ever 💔 fly high king”

item.title: Jaylen Wells on Instagram about Brandon Clarke
item.description: Grizzlies SZN: Jaylen Wells on Instagram about Brandon Clarke.

item.title: Spoke with Brandon Clarke's mentor and former coach …
item.description: Drew Hill: Spoke with Brandon Clarke's mentor and former coach Tony Darden. The AZ basketball community is crushed. Tony shared these videos of his son AJ, who Brandon treated like a little brother. The first from draft night in 2019. "It was really hard to break the news"

item.title: Spurs had a moment of silence for Brandon Clarke and …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Spurs had a moment of silence for Brandon Clarke and Jason Collins. Keldon Johnson came into the NBA at the same time as Clarke. "He seemed like an amazing person and I always heard great things about him," Keldon told me. "It sucks. Sending condolences to his family."

item.title: NBC inexplicably faded to black then cut to commercial …
item.description: The Spurs announcer asks for a moment of silence for the recent passing of Brandon Clarke and Jason Collins. NBC inexplicably faded to black then cut to commercial in the middle of the moment; a DraftK*ngs commercial played on the broadcast.

item.title: “I’m walking with a GOAT right here,” Morant said in …
item.description: In another post, Morant shared a photo of himself with Clarke and Jaren Jackson Jr. Morant later shared a post from Grizzlies social media manager Devin Walker that featured him with Clarke during the 2019 NBA Summer League when he won MVP. “I’m walking with a GOAT right here,” Morant said in the video as the pair walked off the court.

item.title: “Just a really good person, a really good teammate, …
item.description: On Tuesday afternoon, Minnesota Timberwolves forward Kyle Anderson was one of several former and current Memphis players who paid tribute to Clarke. “Just a really good person, a really good teammate, obviously someone who was really good on the court. Just someone I could instantly see and we would start laughing and start sharing jokes. He was Memphis’ own, for sure. He did his part in the community,” Anderson told The Athletic. “It’s just crazy. It really don’t make no sense. I was on the pho

item.title: “He was an incredible player, but an even better …
item.description: Clarke had one year remaining on his contract with Memphis, where he had established himself as a beloved teammate. Niele Ivey, a former Grizzlies assistant coach who is now the head coach of Notre Dame’s women’s basketball team, told The Athletic that she was “heartbroken” to hear of Clarke’s death. “He was an incredible player, but an even better person,” she said. “I have nothing but great memories of his positive energy, enthusiasm and the way he uplifted everyone around him — teammates and

item.title: Jaren Jackson Jr on Brandon Clarke: I couldn’t imagine …
item.description: Jaren Jackson Jr on Brandon Clarke: I couldn’t imagine a world where this was even a possibility. i love you forever brother, this is absolutely devastating. i just wish i could talk to you. you were so much to so many and even more to me . i will never have the words to describe what this feels like . i still can’t believe this . to your family and friends and everyone you were always a light and you were always so genuine and real and our friendship went far beyond anything on the court . i tr

item.title: “I’ve worked out with Memphis and Boston. ... Great …
item.description: Scott Richey: “I’ve worked out with Memphis and Boston. ... Great workouts. Great feedback. I think it's all about evaluating my options and seeing what's the best fit." — @IlliniMBB guard Andrej Stojakovic (@AndrejSto2) on his pre-draft process so far. #Illini #NBADraft

item.title: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in Chicago during the NBA pre-draft camp. Washington is supposed to visit with “multiple players” in DC for the top pick, source days. Dybantsa also met with Utah, Chicago, Sacramento, Brooklyn, Atlanta, Dallas, Memphis & the Clippers.

item.title: Sitting courtside at Oak Ridge to watch the Ones …
item.description: Jason Beede: Sitting courtside at Oak Ridge to watch the Ones Basketball League is former #Magic and current Grizzlies guard Kentavious Caldwell-Pope. @OBLhoops

item.title: The NBA, of course, continues to target the 2027-28 …
item.description: Orlando and Memphis split this season's two Europe games in Berlin in London. Berlin and Paris are scheduled to serve as host cities for the two-game tour in 2028. The NBA, of course, continues to target the 2027-28 season as its launch date for a 12-to-16 team NBA Europe circuit that would be separate from #thisleague. Wemby and the Spurs played two games against Indiana in Paris in January 2025 on the NBA's last in-season visit to France.

item.title: NBA player anonymous poll names Memphis the least desired NBA destination
item.description: When players were asked which team would be at the top of their “no-trade” list, Memphis received more than three times as many votes as the second-place Washington Wizards. "I'll play anywhere, honestly,” one player explained. “But I would have to say Memphis is a place I don't want to live. So that would be the reason why I wouldn't want to play there.” Said another player: “It has nothing to do with the team. It's the location of the team."

item.title: You used to have just the hottest babes in the front …
item.description: Tony Allen: "I'm going to tell you what put a little flame to it. I remember this vividly. I'll tell you what put a little flame. Well, put a little flame to it. You're married now and we can speak on this. You're married now. You used to have just the hottest babes in the front court." Chandler Parsons: "Why was that a bad thing?" Tony Allen: "And then it was that was the focus and then you're rehab, you're rehab somewhere in LA. We look up you in Cabo. It was kind of weird and then it was coac

item.title: Chandler Parsons on Memphis experience: 'I felt like I was in a constant battle with my own home fans and my own front office'
item.description: Chandler Parsons: I appreciate you saying that because again, the shit I had to go through with my injuries and my own personal struggle to get back and to be the player I once was was so difficult. And then you add this pressure of expectation, a big contract, being a missing piece and failing at all of those. It sucked and I just felt not attacked but just I felt like I was in a constant battle with my own home fans and my own front office, my own ownership almost blaming me for taking this co

item.title: Cooper Flagg, Dylan Harper, Kon Knueppel headline All-Rookie First Team
item.description: NBA Communications: The 2025-26 Kia NBA All-Rookie First Team: Cedric Coward, @memgrizz VJ Edgecombe, @sixers Cooper Flagg, @dallasmavs Dylan Harper, @spurs Kon Knueppel, @hornets

item.title: “I appreciate it. It’s been rough. But at the same …
item.description: – I’m sorry about the news with Brandon’s passing. How have you been holding up with everything? Cedric Coward: “I appreciate it. It’s been rough. But at the same time, I’m grateful for the time that I had with him. Now you honor his memory by winning games and doing what he was doing with being a great teammate.”

item.title: He didn’t treat me like the stereotypical vet, giving …
item.description: – What made Brandon such a great teammate? Cedric Coward: “I only had a year with him. But at the same time, it was so impactful due to his love for his teammates. Especially for me as a rookie, he had a lot of love for me. He didn’t treat me like the stereotypical vet, giving me the typical rookie treatment. He did at times, but it was all fun and love. So with him, I thought it was so inspirational that he dealt with so many injuries, but he came in every day with a smile on his face. He came

item.title: “It’s about doing what we can to get better each and …
item.description: – In the spirit of you wanting to honor Brandon’s memory by winning games, how do you go about that? Cedric Coward: “It’s about doing what we can to get better each and every day. He was super impactful, especially during the times that the Grizzlies made the playoffs. So it’s about getting back there. Getting back to the playoffs is huge. It’s also about not taking every day for granted. That’s the biggest thing. You have to understand that time is short, not just for the people that you’re wit

item.title: “Yeah, I’ve talked to KD a lot. After games, I talk to …
item.description: – You’ve also said you hope to ask questions to some of the other star vets, including LeBron [James], Steph [Curry] and KD. Have you been able to do that yet? Cedric Coward: “Yeah, I’ve talked to KD a lot. After games, I talk to him a lot. I haven’t talked to ‘Bron or Steph yet. But the opportunities will come. I was born believing that closed mouths don’t get fed. So whenever I get those opportunities, best believe that I’m going to talk a lot to them about it. But at the end of the day, it’ll

item.title: The Grizzlies hosted a predraft workout today with the …
item.description: Cody Taylor: Source: The Grizzlies hosted a predraft workout today with the following prospects: 🏀 Dion Brown, St. Louis 🏀 MJ Collins, Utah State 🏀 Brendan Hausen, Iowa 🏀 Jaxon Kohler, Michigan State 🏀 Malachi Moreno, Kentucky 🏀 Tounde Yessoufou, Baylor

item.title: Ja Morant is here at the Dream vs. Wings game
item.description: Lauren L. Williams: Grizzlies guard Ja Morant is here at the Dream vs. Wings game.

item.title: Patrick St. Andrews joins Bucks as an assistant coach
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks will add Patrick St. Andrews as an assistant coach, reuniting him with coach Taylor Jenkins. He was an assistant coach for the Blazers. Prior, he was an assistant coach with Jenkins on the Grizzlies and with Mike Budenholzer on the Bucks and Hawks.

item.title: Jovan Buha: “I think there will be competition for …
item.description: Jovan Buha: “I think there will be competition for (Austin Reaves) services. If I'm Brooklyn or Chicago, I think he's, I mean, Brooklyn drafted a million point guards and ball handlers last year, but I don't think any of them project to be as good as Austin Reeves currently is. I think the sweet spot is probably going to be around five years, 200 million. But if you get that number down to around 35 million average annual, that's where I could see someone, Brooklyn, Chicago, Memphis, come in and

item.title: Former Memphis Grizzlies and Sacramento Kings head …
item.description: Peter Hooley: Former Memphis Grizzlies and Sacramento Kings head coach, Dave Joerger, has been interviewed by Melbourne United about their vacant head coaching position Joerger has a strong relationship with Joe Ingles and is currently assistant at the Milwaukee Bucks.

item.title: Dave Joerger in talks to coach Australian team
item.description: Melbourne United are the last team in the League looking for a head coach, and Hools is Hearing that a very prominent and well-respected NBA name is at the top of the list. Current Milwaukee Bucks assistant Dave Joerger has been interviewed by the club recently about potentially taking over after Dean Vickerman’s departure. The 52-year-old has previously been Head Coach of the Memphis Grizzlies and Sacramento Kings, before being added to the Bucks staff two years ago.

item.title: He's the Spur w/at least 13 consecutive points in a …
item.description: Michael C. Wright: Julian Champagnie scored 13 straight from 8:21 to 4:38 in 1Q. He's the Spur w/at least 13 consecutive points in a playoff game since Kawhi Leonard scored 16 straight in Game 4 of the 1st Rd against Memphis in 2017, per ESPN Research.

item.title: Some Grizzlies draft workouts today, per league …
item.description: Damichael Cole: Some Grizzlies draft workouts today, per league sources: J’Vonne Hadley (Louisville); Sam Alexis (Indiana); Chase Ross (Marquette): Tyler Tanner (Vanderbilt)

item.title: On average, top-five picks from 2000 through 2019 …
item.description: The dream of a decade-plus of production for a franchise cornerstone also doesn't materialize very often. On average, top-five picks from 2000 through 2019 lasted 5.1 seasons with their original team, with 56% lasting until their fifth year (and their second contract) and 44% leaving before then. Only 6% played at least 10 seasons with their initial team. (Tatum will bump that figure to 7% next season.) And for now, only five of the 100 top-five picks in that period are still with the team that

item.title: On Ja’s IG story…
item.description: Mark Jones: On Ja’s IG story…

item.title: The NBA's Board of Governors vote passed 29-1 -- with …
item.description: Shams Charania: The NBA's Board of Governors vote passed 29-1 -- with the Memphis Grizzlies as the lone vote against the new anti-tanking lottery reform, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Grizzlies opposed lottery reform over rule barring teams from three consecutive top-5 picks
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources said the Grizzlies voted against the new lottery reform solely because of the new rule disallowing a team from picking top 5 in 3 consecutive years. As @BobbyMarks42 says, the Grizzlies have the more favorable 2027 first-rounder of Cleveland, Minnesota and Utah, and because the Jazz again have a top-5 pick this year, their first in 2027 cannot be in the top-5 under new rules.

item.title: As I currently understand it, if the Jazz's 2027 …
item.description: Jake Fischer: As I currently understand it, if the Jazz's 2027 first-rounder that's owed to Memphis, and can't be top-five next year, were to land at No. 5, it would be slated at No. 6. But, there's also the possibility that the league would just discard that outcome and draw again for No. 5.

item.title: According to NBA president of league operations Byron …
item.description: According to NBA president of league operations Byron Spruell, Grizzlies general manager Zach Kleiman and team president Jason Wexler were both heavily involved in the process. Kleiman played an active role in the league’s general manager committee discussions, while Wexler participated in Board of Governors meetings, where Memphis ultimately became the lone organization to vote against the proposal.

item.title: "If you were to grandfather in already traded picks, …
item.description: While the reform is designed to further discourage tanking and flatten lottery odds across the league, Memphis appears to be the franchise most directly affected by one of its unintended consequences. Evan Wasch, NBA executive vice president, head of basketball strategy and growth, explained how the restrictions with the Utah pick travels to the acquiring team in the new system. The league didn't want to create an imbalance in value on traded and non-traded picks. "If you were to grandfather in
